basics.name: Chu-Rong Chen
basics.headline: Incoming M.S. Robotics student at Georgia Tech

info: Bachelor of Science in Engineering – Electrical Engineering

work.name: Ambarella Inc.
work.position: Software Engineering Intern - Embedded AI, Algorithm, Tools
highlights: Built a dynamic weight adaptation mechanism for motion detection CNNs, designing convolutional LoRA adapters conditioned on ISP metadata.
Optimized the model training pipeline to generalize across varying camera settings, cutting training time by ~20% while maintaining high accuracy.
Stabilized model convergence, reducing evaluation loss variance by 90% across diverse camera settings.

work.name: Institute of Information Science, Academia Sinica
highlights: Accelerated a Post-Quantum Cryptography (PQC) signature scheme by implementing vectorized AVX2 instruction sets (SIMD) on x86-64 architecture.
Achieved a 5× speedup while enforcing constant-time execution to mitigate timing-based side-channel attacks.

work.name: Vision & Learning Lab, NTUEE
highlights: Engineered a geometric initialization scheme utilizing monocular depth estimation to enhance the surface reconstruction fidelity of sparse voxel octrees.

work.name: Robot Learning Lab, NTUEE
highlights: Architected a sim-to-real transfer pipeline using differentiable simulation for online physical parameter estimation in contact-rich robotic tasks.

Designed a framework leveraging optical flow as action primitives to learn policies from action-free videos, enabling zero-shot cross-embodiment transfer; deployed the algorithm on the Mobile ALOHA bi-manual manipulator and established a robust real-world data collection pipeline.

work.name: Teaching Assistant, NTUEE
highlights:
Cornerstone EECS Design and Implementation (Spring 2023 - 2024):
Mentored freshman students in system engineering projects, integrating the hardware (Arduino) and software (Python) for autonomous mechatronics.

Signals and Systems (Spring 2024):
Facilitated student learning in signal processing fundamentals, Fourier analysis, and linear system theory through tutorial sessions and technical support.

projects.name: Multimodal Perception of Corner Cases in Autonomous Driving
highlights: Created a multimodal pipeline for autonomous driving using the CODA-LM dataset, targeting global scene understanding and local area reasoning.
Fine-tuned LLaVA-1.5 7B via LoRA, integrating custom modules to resolve edge cases such as small object detection and complex traffic scenarios.

projects.name: NTUEE LightDance
highlights: Led a 10-member team to build a full-stack light effect editor, delivering ~5× the performance of the legacy JavaScript system.
Built a high-concurrency Rust backend managing complex SQL operations, supporting simultaneous editing across 5,000+ LEDs and 1,000+ keyframes.
Developed a custom Blender add-on for real-time 3D visualization, enabling designers to preview light effects in a physically accurate environment.

projects.name: Automatic Frisbee Shooter
highlights: Prototyped a fully automated 4-Wheel-Drive robot using ROS 2 on Raspberry Pi, integrating navigation and actuation sub-systems with a PID control loop.
Engineered a computer vision tracking system to calculate frisbee angle and velocity based on real-time target data.

projects.name: FPGA Laser Shooter
highlights: Designed a hardware-accelerated laser shooting system in Verilog, achieving low-latency target acquisition and firing control.
Implemented a pipelined Hough Circle Transform architecture on FPGA for real-time target detection at high frame rates.

projects.name: MusicTracker - Automatic Music Sheet Page Turner
highlights: Developed a real-time audio processing system on STM32 using DMA and I2S, leveraging the CMSIS DSP library for Mel spectrogram analysis.
Implemented a Dynamic Time Warping (DTW) algorithm to synchronize live audio with sheet music, triggering a servo-actuated page turner.

projects.name: MoneyManager - Personal Finance Tracker
highlights: Built a cross-platform mobile application for finance tracking using React Native, featuring intuitive data visualization for income and expenses.
Implemented a RESTful backend with Express.js and MongoDB to handle user authentication and secure data persistence.

projects.name: MKS Access System - Entrance Recording System
highlights: Engineered an IoT access control system using C++ for peripheral management (ID scanner, LCD) on a Raspberry Pi.
Integrated a server-side backend to log student entry data and display real-time usage instructions.

projects.name: Furuta Pendulum - Digital Controller Design
highlights: Modeled and linearized the dynamics of a Furuta pendulum to design an optimal LQR controller in Simulink.
Deployed the control algorithm to physical hardware, achieving stable equilibrium and robust disturbance rejection.

projects.name: Ear Detection Gate - Ear Shape Identification System
highlights: Developed a contactless biometric scanning system using YOLO object detection to identify individuals by ear shape.
Designed the system to function effectively without mask removal, addressing specific security challenges during the COVID-19 pandemic.

projects.name: Intelligent Shopping Cart - Indoor Navigation and Checkout System
highlights: Created a shopping cart system featuring BLE-based indoor localization and product guidance; awarded Taiwan Utility Model Patent.

projects.name: CPLC Lander Challenge - Student Self-landing Rocket
highlights: Contributed to the avionics team for a VTVL (Vertical Takeoff, Vertical Landing) rocket, assisting in system design and specification.
Designed the universal prototype controller board to interface with various sensors and actuators used in the rocket.

projects.name: Kibo RPC - International Space Robotics Challenge
highlights: Secured the World Championship representing Taiwan, with code successfully deployed on JAXA's Astrobee robot aboard the International Space Station.
Developed a vision-based laser aiming system and a trajectory planning engine to autonomously navigate obstacle-filled "Keep-Out Zones" with high accuracy and safety.

publications.name: Learning Skill Abstraction from Action-Free Videos via Optical Flow

venue.name: ICML 2025 Workshop on Building Physically Plausible World Models

highlights: Directed the academic division in curating technical workshops, including embedded systems tutorials, PCB design, and career seminars for the EE student body.
Oversaw the operations of the department MakerSpace, managing equipment maintenance and resource allocation to support student innovation projects.

volunteer.organization: NTUEE Camp - Summer Camp for High School Students
highlights: Led a 20-member teaching team to develop a comprehensive engineering curriculum, designing hands-on software (Python) and hardware (Arduino) projects for 120+ high school students across Taiwan.
Constructed custom bipedal combat robots for regional competitions, focusing on structural stability and actuator control.
Co-founded the high school's inaugural robotics competition team, establishing a framework for future participation in national contests.
